node.statement: (Generalized vs ordinary affine maps). When f_j are ordinary continuous affine functionals (f_j(x) ∈ R for all x ∈ K, no +∞ values), the lower semicontinuity and affinity conditions of the Weis-Shirokov theorem are automatically satisfied. This is the case when the constraints are φ(a_j) = β_j for bounded self-adjoint elements a_j ∈ A^{sa}. The generalized version (allowing +∞) is needed for unbounded observables (e.g., Hamiltonians on infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces: f(ρ) = Tr(Hρ) may be +∞ for some density operators ρ).
